# Self-Improvement Tasks — ~/.claude Configuration
<!-- Generated by /self-improve on 2026-06-09 (Fable model run).
     Review each item, remove any you don't want,
     then run: /plan-mission implement the tasks in code-review-tasks.md -->

> **Fable 5 access expires 2026-06-22.** Before that date, revert `settings.json`
> `"model"` to `"claude-sonnet-4-6"` (or remove the line to use the account default).
> Also update any agent frontmatter that was set to `model: fable` during this session.

## Must fix (Critical)

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md:77-81` — Fable 5 missing from model routing table. GA as of
  today: 1M native context, 128K output, $10/$50/MTok (2× Opus), effort `high` default,
  purpose-built for long-horizon autonomous execution (mission briefs, multi-hour/multi-day
  sessions). Fix: add row for Fable 5 with purpose "Mission-brief execution, autonomous
  sessions." Also add a Fable 5 behavioral note alongside the Opus compensation block
  (see Suggestion item "Fable 5 behavioral notes").

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md:85` — Factual error: "Haiku 4.5 does not support adaptive
  thinking or extended thinking; effort levels are ignored." Verified wrong on two counts:
  (a) Haiku 4.5 DOES support fixed-budget extended thinking (`budget_tokens`); (b) the
  `effort` parameter returns HTTP 400 on Haiku 4.5 — it is not silently ignored.
  Fix: "Haiku 4.5 supports fixed-budget extended thinking (`budget_tokens`) but not
  adaptive thinking; the `effort` parameter returns 400 on Haiku — do not set it."

- [ ] `templates/autonomous-settings.json:38-42` and `.claude/settings.json:38-42` —
  `Read(*)`, `Write(*)`, `Edit(*)`, `Glob(*)`, `Grep(*)` use single-segment glob. In
  Claude Code permission rules `*` matches one path segment and won't match
  `src/api/users.ts`, effectively blocking all nested file operations in autonomous mode.
  Fix: change all five to `Read(**)`, `Write(**)`, `Edit(**)`, `Glob(**)`, `Grep(**)`.

- [ ] `skills/self-improve/SKILL.md:415,:494` — references
  `~/.claude/agents/09-meta-orchestration/memory-curator.md` which was deleted (confirmed
  by git status `D`). The `cat` call at line 494 fails every self-improve run.
  Fix: replace both references with an existing agent path or remove the step entirely.

## Should fix (Warning)

- [ ] `settings.json:259` — `"model": "claude-fable-5[1m]"`. Fable 5's native context is
  1M; the `[1m]` suffix is Claude Code's opt-in for models whose default window is smaller.
  It is redundant on a natively-1M model and may cause a parse error after a format update.
  Fix: change to `"model": "claude-fable-5"`.

- [ ] `.mcp.json` (working tree) vs committed state — mem0 was migrated to http transport
  (commit dd03d0c), but the working tree replaced the file with serena-only, orphaning mem0.
  `.claude/settings.local.json:36` still has `"enabledMcpjsonServers": ["mem0"]` and
  `templates/autonomous-settings.json` still has 5 `mcp__mem0__*` permissions.
  Fix: decide: (a) restore mem0 alongside serena in `.mcp.json` and commit, or (b) finish
  the removal — drop the `enabledMcpjsonServers` entry and the 5 template permissions.
  Also fix `hooks/project-init.sh` to merge rather than overwrite `.mcp.json` so future
  removals don't recreate this issue.

- [ ] `skills/self-improve/research-urls.md:33-38,70` — 7 `platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/
  claude-code/*` URLs return 404; Claude Code docs moved to `code.claude.com/docs/en/`.
  The tutorials URL (line 70) uses the same dead base. All 7 now marked `unreachable` in
  the registry (updated in Phase 6 of this run).
  Fix: find replacement URLs at `code.claude.com/docs/en/` for each page (overview, hooks,
  settings, memory, mcp, sub-agents, tutorials) and promote them from Candidate URLs once
  they pass the thin-content bar (≥1000 chars).

- [ ] `CLAUDE.md:12` — "use Context7 before answering" — no Context7 MCP server is
  configured in `.mcp.json` or `settings.json`. Every session gets an instruction for a
  tool that does not exist.
  Fix: change to "read the official docs (WebFetch)" or reinstall the Context7 MCP server.

- [ ] `rules/prompting-quality.md:88-91` — arxiv:2604.00025 citation has two errors: (a)
  arxiv IDs starting with 2604 are April 2026 submissions — the entry labels it "2025";
  (b) "26pp accuracy loss on planning tasks" — the paper actually studies math/science
  benchmarks on general LLMs, not planning tasks or Opus-tier agents specifically.
  Fix: correct year to 2026; reword finding to: "26pp accuracy gain from brevity constraints
  on math/science benchmarks across 31 general LLMs (preprint, not planning-task-specific)."

- [ ] `rules/prompting-quality.md:44-51` — "effective context degrades past ~20K tokens,
  per arxiv:2509.21361" — the cited paper reports degradation at ~1K tokens, not 20K.
  The 20K figure is unsourced.
  Fix: remove the specific 20K threshold; retain the practical caps (20-30 files per agent,
  pass line ranges not full files) as sound heuristics without the unsourced number.

- [ ] `post-compact-context.md:30` and missing sections — model routing omits Fable 5;
  Conventional Commits format is never restored after compaction.
  Fix: (a) update line 30 to include Fable→long-horizon autonomous execution;
  (b) add a "## Commit Format (restored)" section: type(scope): description ≤72 chars,
  lowercase, no period; body explains why for >3-file changes.

- [ ] `agents/04-quality-security/security-auditor.md:5-6` — `model: haiku` for a
  comprehensive security/compliance audit. Haiku is the scoring/dedup tier — not threat
  modeling. Also `effort: high` returns HTTP 400 on Haiku 4.5.
  Fix: change to `model: sonnet` (minimum for code analysis); remove `effort: high`;
  add `security.md` to Required Rules.

- [ ] `.claude/settings.json` (nested) — duplicates 6 hook events already in global
  `settings.json` (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostCompact, PreCompact, PostToolUse,
  Stop). When cwd is `~/.claude` every hook fires twice: double chime, double post-compact
  injection (~2,200 tokens wasted per compaction).
  Fix: remove the `hooks` block from `.claude/settings.json`; keep only the autonomous
  permission delta not already in global settings.

- [ ] `hooks/autonomous-toggle.sh:45-48` — running `on` a second time backs up the already-
  autonomous settings.json, permanently overwriting `settings.pre-autonomous.json` with
  autonomous content (original settings permanently lost).
  Fix: add guard before backup: `if cmp -s "$SETTINGS_FILE" "$SOURCE"; then
  echo "Already in autonomous mode"; exit 0; fi`

- [ ] `templates/autonomous-settings.json` — missing `Bash(~/.claude/hooks/quality-gate.sh:*)`
  permission. `autonomous-execution.md` mandates quality-gate.sh between every batch, but
  the template blocks it, causing a permission prompt mid-mission.
  Fix: add the entry to the template's allow list.

- [ ] `settings.json` — 3 Serena tools present in the autonomous template but missing from
  global settings: `mcp__serena__find_referencing_symbols`, `mcp__serena__safe_delete_symbol`,
  `mcp__serena__rename_symbol`. These are in `rules/lsp.md` as required subagent tools.
  Fix: add all three to the global `settings.json` allow list.

- [ ] `settings.json:116` — `"WebFetch(*)"` is non-canonical; the allow-all form is bare
  `"WebFetch"`. The `(*)` form may not match tool calls depending on the permission parser.
  Fix: change to `"WebFetch"` (bare, no argument).

- [ ] `agents/01-core-development/api-designer.md:9-77,139-177`,
  `agents/04-quality-security/architect-reviewer.md:13-141`,
  `agents/04-quality-security/code-reviewer.md:21-119` — ~110, ~128, and ~99 lines of
  two-word noun-phrase bullets ("Content negotiation", "Technology maturity",
  "Readability analysis") with no directives, thresholds, or pass/fail criteria.
  ~1,850 tokens loaded per invocation that change no model behavior.
  Fix: delete the noun-phrase bullet sections in all three agents; retain only the mission
  statement (1-2 lines), measurable checklist, and Required Rules section.

- [ ] `post-compact-context.md:17-110` — 94 lines restore digests of rule files that are
  already injected as instruction context every session. CLAUDE.md:34-36 says this file
  exists "for content that isn't in any instruction file" — current content contradicts that.
  ~1,100 tokens wasted per compaction.
  Fix: trim to: (a) autonomous recovery sequence (current lines 6-15, keep as-is),
  (b) model routing + commit format (from Warning above), (c) any session-only state
  genuinely not covered by rule files. Target: ~20-25 lines total.

- [ ] `skills/auth-setup/SKILL.md` — only production-infrastructure setup skill missing an
  Operational Readiness section (analytics, compliance, and payments all got sections in
  commit 824bd4d). Auth is the highest-risk surface area.
  Fix: add section mirroring payments-setup pattern: SLIs (login success rate, session
  validation latency, OAuth callback error rate), 2-3 failure modes with detection and
  mitigation, rollback classification (reversible).

- [ ] `skills/plan-mission/SKILL.md` — generated mission briefs have no Fable 5 executor
  recommendation. Mission briefs drive long-horizon autonomous execution — exactly Fable 5's
  intended use case — but neither the model routing table nor the Phase 8 handoff mentions it.
  Fix: add Fable 5 to the brief template's model routing guidance and to Phase 8:
  "Recommended execution model: claude-fable-5 (long-horizon, native 1M context)."

- [ ] `skills/project-bootstrap/SKILL.md` — orchestrates 6 sub-skills with no resumability.
  On failure, there is no record of position, collected inputs, or completed skills.
  Fix: write `.bootstrap-progress.md` after Step 4 (inputs collected) and after each
  completed sub-skill; check on startup and resume from first incomplete. Add failure
  policy: "if a skill's verify step fails, stop and report — do not start the next skill."

- [ ] `settings.json` — permission list has 16 redundant subsumed entries (e.g.,
  `Bash(npx eslint:*)` subsumed by `Bash(npx:*)`; `Bash(uv run:*)` by `Bash(uv:*)`;
  `Bash(pip install:*)` by `Bash(pip:*)`). Also missing common read-only commands that
  generate daily prompts.
  Fix: (a) remove the 16 subsumed entries; (b) add `"Bash(cat:*)"`, `"Bash(head:*)"`,
  `"Bash(tail:*)"`, `"Bash(wc:*)"`, `"Bash(diff:*)"`.

- [ ] `agents/04-quality-security/code-reviewer.md:13` — claims "Code coverage ≥ 90% line/
  branch/function confirmed" but the agent has `disallowedTools: Write, Edit, Bash` — it
  cannot run a coverage tool. The claim is structurally impossible.
  Fix: reword to: "verify a coverage report exists in CI output; flag uncovered changed
  lines; do not claim a coverage percentage without an attached report."

- [ ] `agents/01-core-development/backend-developer.md:56-65` — Required Rules block missing
  `security.md` (despite declaring OWASP compliance "non-negotiable") and
  `retry-idempotency.md` (despite mandating idempotency on queue consumers).
  Fix: add both files to Required Rules.

- [ ] `rules/prompting-quality.md:3-14` — "use at least one constraint keyword including
  must/always/never" — Anthropic guidance for Claude 4.6+/Fable 5 is that blanket intensity
  words (CRITICAL/MUST/ALWAYS) overtrigger on these models; scoping keywords ("only," "limit
  to," "do not touch X") remain effective on all tiers.
  Fix: add after the constraint keywords list: "On Claude 4.6+/Fable 5, prefer scoping
  keywords over intensity escalation; CRITICAL/MUST/ALWAYS can overtrigger and degrade output."

- [ ] `agents/04-quality-security/architect-reviewer.md:143-175` — inlines
  `rules/architecture.md` verbatim (~430 tokens: blast-radius layers, ADR triggers,
  reversibility, fitness functions, breaking-change taxonomy) — duplicates already-loaded
  instruction context on every invocation.
  Fix: replace lines 143-175 with `Execute per ~/.claude/rules/architecture.md`; add
  `architecture.md` to Required Rules (following commit 68836c0 pattern).

## Consider improving (Suggestion)

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md:99-104` — Opus behavioral compensation (no infer, no over-
  engineer, no spawn subagents) is correct for Opus 4.8 but WRONG for Fable 5. Fable 5
  encourages frequent parallel subagent dispatch; too-prescriptive skills actively degrade
  its output below baseline.
  Fix: add Fable 5 note alongside the Opus block: "When routing to Fable 5: describe the
  outcome, not the steps; omit verification reminders; subagent dispatch is encouraged."

- [ ] `skills/code-review/SKILL.md:108-152` — Security agent section has ~17 parallel
  prescriptive constraints. Per MOSAIC research (arxiv:2601.18554), >15 constraints per
  section produces unpredictable/averaged compliance.
  Fix: split into sub-sections "Auth coverage" / "Injection & input" / "Secrets & data
  exposure" / "Transport & headers", each ≤6 items.

- [ ] `agents/02-language-specialists/typescript-pro.md:35-53` — "Banned patterns" section
  has 14 items in one block (MOSAIC 7-15 band = unpredictable compliance).
  Fix: split into "Compiler flag bans" (≤6) and "Module & syntax bans" (≤6) sub-sections.

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md:115` — "scope to 3-5 tools per agent" anti-pattern rule is
  violated by every agent definition (10-21 tools each). The rule is dead as written.
  Fix: add: "Serena MCP navigation tools count as one navigation capability for this limit,
  not 8+ individual entries."

- [ ] `rules/lsp.md:12-80` — subagent guidance appears three times (blockquote, diagnostics
  closing, and Subagent note). ~120 redundant tokens on every file load.
  Fix: keep only the Subagent note section; reduce blockquote to one line; delete the
  duplicated diagnostics guidance.

- [ ] 12 multi-step skills missing resumability: auth-setup, payments-setup, testing-setup,
  i18n-setup, brand-knowvah, powerpoint-addin-setup, code-review, review-pr, upgrade-deps,
  self-improve, explore, compliance-setup. An interruption leaves no position record.
  Fix: establish a pattern — write `.{skill-name}-progress.md` after input-gathering and
  after each major step; check on startup and resume from first incomplete step. Start with
  production-infra skills (auth-setup in Warning above; payments, testing, i18n next).

- [ ] 7 skills missing YAML frontmatter (auth-setup, analytics-setup, payments-setup,
  testing-setup, project-bootstrap, powerpoint-addin-setup, brand-knowvah). Inconsistent
  with the other 21 skills.
  Fix: add `description:` and `allowed-tools:` frontmatter to each.

- [ ] `skills/testing-setup/SKILL.md:Step13.5` — "Commit the changes and push" — pushing
  without user approval violates the global rule.
  Fix: "offer to commit; push only with explicit user approval."

- [ ] `rules/architecture.md` + `rules/api-design.md` — breaking-change taxonomy duplicated
  in both files (~50 redundant tokens).
  Fix: keep full taxonomy in architecture.md; replace api-design.md section with one line:
  "Increment major version on any breaking change per `~/.claude/rules/architecture.md`."

## Notes (low-friction fixes)

- [ ] `skills/self-improve/SKILL.md` Phase 0 — step numbering has gap: 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 (no 3).
  Fix: renumber sequentially 1–6.

- [ ] `skills/compliance-setup/SKILL.md:Step7` — two items numbered "2.". Fix: renumber.

- [ ] `rules/prompting-quality.md:88` — label "Hakim, 2025" should be "Hakim, 2026"
  (arxiv ID 2604.xxxxx = April 2026 submission month, not 2025).

- [ ] `hooks/project-init.sh` — `trap 'exit 0' ERR` swallows all errors silently.
  Fix: add at minimum line-number logging before the exit:
  `trap 'echo "project-init error: line $LINENO" >> ~/.claude/logs/project-init.err' ERR`

---

## How to pre-approve the Agent tool

To stop per-call permission prompts when Claude launches subagents, add
`"Agent(*)"` to the `permissions.allow` array in `~/.claude/settings.json`,
in the same list as the existing `Bash(...)` and `mcp__serena__*` entries.
